seoTitle: How to Customize Zendesk Help Center Branding
metaDescription: Learn how to customize your Zendesk Help Center's branding and layout using the no-code theme designer or the underlying CSS code editor.
intro: A self-service portal that still looks like the default Zendesk template can undercut trust in your brand. This guide walks through customizing the Help Center's logo, colors, typography, and layout using both the no-code designer and the direct code editor for finer control.

steps.heading: Open Customize Design
steps.body: In Knowledge Admin, click the Customize Design sidebar icon and select Themes.

steps.heading: Launch the visual editor
steps.body: Click Customize Theme to open the no-code brand options panel.

steps.heading: Upload logos and fonts
steps.body: Add your corporate logo, favicon, and adjust global typography fonts.

steps.heading: Match your brand palette
steps.body: Set your main palette colors and toggle homepage section column display settings.

steps.heading: Use the code editor for advanced control
steps.body: Click Edit Code to open the CSS stylesheet and template files if you need custom structural changes.

steps.heading: Preview across devices and publish
steps.body: Use the sandbox preview tool to verify layouts on multiple screen sizes, then click Publish and set the theme live.

faq.question: Can I revert to my previous design if something breaks?
faq.answer: Yes, downloading a rollback backup ZIP archive before making changes lets you restore the earlier version.

faq.question: Do design changes affect published article content?
faq.answer: No, styling changes apply globally without modifying your underlying database articles.

faq.question: Is CSS knowledge required for basic branding?
faq.answer: No, the no-code theme editor covers logos, colors, and fonts without touching code.

faq.question: How do I check mobile responsiveness before publishing?
faq.answer: The interactive sandbox preview tool lets you check your layout across different screen sizes before going live.
